program.name: CommBank Graduate, Intern and Analyst Programs
locations: Australia (locations vary by stream; majority of roles based in Sydney; some roles in other cities and regional areas)
Global Markets Summer Analyst Program: Sydney
Technology Associate Program: Tech Hubs across Australia
program.duration_text: Graduate Program: 12–24 months depending on the program, and most programs run for 18 months. Summer Intern Program: 10 weeks. Global Markets Summer Analyst Program: 10 weeks (late November to early February).
program.rotation_text: Graduate Program: rotations across different areas during the program. Risk Management pathway example: three rotations over 18 months. Some pathways describe three to four rotations over 12–15 months (varies by pathway).

eligibility.work_rights: For the Graduate Program, Summer Intern Program, and Global Markets Summer Analyst Program, you must be an Australian or New Zealand citizen, or an Australian permanent resident at the time you apply. Temporary visa holders are not eligible. Bridging visas while awaiting permanent residency and 820 visa holders are not included.
eligibility.graduation_window: Graduate Program: you must be in your final year of an undergraduate or postgraduate degree, or have completed university studies in the last 24 months. You must have completed your studies and be available to start full-time work in mid-February. Summer Intern Program and Global Markets Summer Analyst Program: you must be in your second last year of an undergraduate or postgraduate degree. Technology Associate Program: you completed your most recent tech qualification in the last 12 months or are due to complete it within the next 3 months, and you have less than 12 months of tech work experience.
disciplines: Graduate Program and Summer Intern Program: university undergraduate or postgraduate degree (specific disciplines not listed on the overview page).

Technology Associate Program: alternative education pathways aligned to Engineering, Data Science or Cybersecurity, including TAFE courses, traineeships, micro-credentials, bootcamps and vendor certifications.
minimum_requirements: Graduate Program: overall credit average of 65 WAM/GPA (70 WAM/GPA for Technology: Engineering + Cyber Security).
Summer Intern Program: overall credit average of 65 WAM/GPA (70 WAM/GPA for Technology: Engineering + Cyber Security).
Global Markets Summer Analyst Program: overall credit average of 65 WAM/GPA.
Global Markets Summer Analyst Program: you must be living in Australia when you apply.
Technology Associate Program: if you want part-time work, you must be available at least two days per week.

stages.name: Apply online
stages.details: Submit an online application form. You will select one career pathway and a stream/program preference. You are encouraged to have an updated resume and your most recent transcript ready.

stages.name: Online assessment
stages.details: If your application is successful, you may be invited to complete an online assessment. After you complete it, you receive insights about your capabilities.

stages.name: Digital interview
stages.details: If you progress, you may complete a digital interview with short pre-recorded questions. You respond using the front-facing camera on your computer or mobile. The email invite includes instructions and a 48-hour deadline.

stages.name: Assessment centre
stages.details: If successful at digital interview, you may be invited to a virtual or face-to-face assessment centre, where you can meet people from the business and ask questions. For the Global Markets Summer Analyst Program, this stage can differ: shortlisted applicants may be invited to virtual interviews with specific teams, sometimes with an additional coding assessment, and there may be two interview rounds.

tips: Have your updated resume and most recent transcript ready before you apply.
Monitor your email after each stage. If you do not get an application confirmation email within 24–48 hours, check your junk folder and add the CommBank email address as a known sender.
Be ready to complete the digital interview within the 48-hour deadline once you receive the invite.
Choose your career pathway carefully because you can only select one pathway preference in the online application.

exec_themes: “Build tomorrow’s bank today” by investing for the long term, especially in technology and frontline teams to improve customer experience and keep services resilient.
Keeping customers safe (scams, fraud and cyber security) as a core strategic priority, including major investment and use of AI to prevent losses.
Staying at the centre of customers’ financial lives (being the main bank for customers) and deepening relationships across retail and business banking.
Disciplined execution and conservative balance sheet settings (capital, funding, provisioning and interest rate risk) to stay resilient through economic cycles.
Supporting Australia’s future economy, including helping households and businesses and lending to “productive parts of the economy.”
Embedding sustainability into strategy, with a focus on supporting the transition to net zero by 2050 and improving climate disclosures and reporting.
commercial_context.profit_engine: CommBank mainly makes money from traditional banking: earning net interest income on lending funded by deposits, supported by volume growth in core lending and deposits. It also aims to sustain shareholder returns through disciplined cost control and capital management (dividends and share buy-backs), while continuing to invest in technology and customer service.
commercial_context.headwinds: Competition is putting pressure on pricing (especially in home lending and deposit pricing), which can weigh on net interest margin. Costs are rising due to inflation and accelerated investment, lifting operating expenses. Management also highlights macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainty as risks, and notes conditions remain challenging for some customers.
commercial_context.esg: CommBank says it is embedding environmental and social issues into its strategy and reporting, and it states a commitment to support Australia’s transition to a net zero economy by 2050. It publishes standalone climate disclosures and notes the need to improve disclosures in line with upcoming mandatory climate reporting requirements. It also frames customer safety (scams/fraud), accessibility, inclusion and diversity, community investment, and cyber security as key sustainability initiatives.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Greater emphasis on AI at an organisation-wide level (with a stated focus on responsible AI), including using AI for scam/fraud detection and customer protection, alongside continued investment in technology and frontline teams. It is also strengthening climate reporting/disclosures in preparation for mandatory climate-related financial reporting requirements.

notes: Program structure, key dates (months), and locations are taken from the CommBank Graduate & Intern Programs overview page.
Some pathway-specific duration/rotation details are shown on pathway pages (for example, Risk Management).

The Technology Associate Program has different eligibility rules, listed on the same overview page.

notes: Recruitment stages and practical tips (including the 48-hour digital interview window and the 24–48 hour confirmation email guidance) are stated on the CommBank Graduate & Intern Programs overview page.

sources.title: Our Graduate and Intern Programs - CommBank

notes: Exec themes and strategy statements are explicit on CommBank investor and sustainability pages, but some items (e.g., ‘profit engine’ phrasing) are simplified summaries of multiple report statements rather than a single explicit sentence.

Headwinds are inferred from explicit commentary on competition impacts on margin, higher operating expenses, and macro/geopolitical uncertainty. Not all sources label these as ‘headwinds,’ so wording is kept plain and close to the underlying statements.

‘Recent pivot’ is based on Newsroom announcements and investor result commentary that emphasise AI/technology investment and responsible AI; this may be an evolution rather than a single dated strategic pivot.
